requirements.title: Forge-controlled UI boot
acceptance: Loading the generated module alone does not mount the Prompt Agent UI.
The UI connects when the Forge host becomes available after the bundle loads.
requirements.assertion_policy: Observe mount and connection state; do not lock incidental DOM nesting.

requirements.title: Responsive floating windows and focus
acceptance: Chat and settings remain fully inside the current viewport.
Phone settings use the persisted floating layout rather than a forced full-screen layout.
Tablet and desktop may show chat and settings together.
Focusing the chat composer brings chat forward and permits continuous input.
requirements.assertion_policy: Assert containment, floating behavior, visibility, focus, and input continuity. Exact coordinates or dimensions require an explicit acceptance statement.

requirements.title: Local model loading feedback
acceptance: On-demand local model startup shows a distinct loading phase and elapsed time.
Completion, failure, and cancellation clear the loading state without leaving the composer disabled.
requirements.assertion_policy: Assert user-visible phase and recovery, not polling count or timer implementation.

requirements.title: Terminal request recovery
acceptance: Provider failure and user abort both reach a terminal runtime state.
The active request is cleared and the composer can submit again.
requirements.assertion_policy: Assert terminal state and later usability; do not depend on private promise ordering.

requirements.title: Refresh interruption without replay
acceptance: Partial assistant content is persisted before refresh.
Unfinished work becomes interrupted and no old provider request or tool call resumes.
A later submission succeeds.
requirements.assertion_policy: Assert durable transcript and request/tool counts across reload.

requirements.title: Verified named-entity background lookup
acceptance: Named-entity background questions first search and inspect local Forge styles.
Danbooru lookup is used only when no local style matches.
Unverified text is hidden and cannot become the persisted answer.
requirements.assertion_policy: Assert forced tool sequence, source fallback, and visible transcript semantics.

requirements.title: Forge tool authority and freshness
acceptance: The agent receives only the declared Forge tool surface.
Browser tool arguments are revalidated by Python.
Prompt and generation mutations require fresh hashes.
requirements.assertion_policy: Exact tool names are contractual; internal registry implementation is not.

requirements.title: Provider-native tool execution contract
acceptance: OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic, Gemini, and llama.cpp requests use their native forced-tool format.
Tool-call streams normalize to one frontend event contract.
Cancellation closes upstream work.
requirements.assertion_policy: Provider wire fields are contractual; request ordering outside the protocol is not.

requirements.title: On-demand llama.cpp lifecycle
acceptance: Runtime status exposes only idle, loading, ready, or failed plus elapsed time.
Force stop during loading reclaims the process.
Status and stop responses do not expose local paths or endpoints.
requirements.assertion_policy: Assert lifecycle phases, process reclamation, and safe public projection.

requirements.title: Secrets and local paths remain server-owned
acceptance: Public profile and error responses never contain plaintext secrets, provider endpoints, or local paths.
Browser stream requests cannot inject server-owned credentials, endpoints, models, headers, or paths.
UNC and untrusted executable/model paths are rejected before filesystem access.
requirements.assertion_policy: Assert absence of sensitive values and rejection before privileged access.

requirements.title: Stale writes and replay are prevented
acceptance: Prompt and generation writes require the latest server-validated hash.
Stale tool feedback can be refreshed and retried without repeating the same blind write.
Refresh never replays an old tool call.
requirements.assertion_policy: Assert hash validation, corrected retry, and no replay.
